extraction.one_line_summary: MOHORIČ won from a 29-rider breakaway including VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT, gaining 30 seconds on POGAČAR and triggering GC reshuffling.
tactical_patterns: UAE allowed VAN AERT 30-second advantage despite explicit warnings about his climbing threat from prior Tour performances.
VAN DER POEL's yellow jersey team allowed 29-rider mega-break, gambling breakaway riders eliminate each other on climb.
CAVENDISH infiltrated break solely for 20 green jersey points, revealing sprint competition prioritization over stage hunting.
YATES and NIBALI joined break targeting Signal d'Uchon's KOM points/bonuses, indicating GC ambitions via time bonuses.

rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: First Tour stage win in long hard stages without massive climbs; excels when attrition matters
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Gained 30s on GC podium contenders; commentary identifies him as climbing threat UAE underestimated
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: Retained yellow by 30s but losing time suggests one climb too many tomorrow
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Tactical masterclass joining break for points not stage; extended green jersey lead when rivals scored zero
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Jumbo shifting leadership to him over ROGLIČ after today; positioned 2nd among GC contenders
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: Dropped to 5th on GC after break succeeded; UAE's passive tactics backfired
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Unexpected break inclusion showed residual podium ambition via time bonuses at 11km climb
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Break participation signals hunting KOM points and time bonuses rather than defending position
rider_signals.LATOUR Pierre: positive: Commentary identifies him as current podium projection after time gains
